articles.title: B.C. teacher disciplined after students showed up at her home and consumed alcohol in front of her
articles.description: A B.C. teacher has agreed to a one-day suspension and remedial education after she “consumed a shot of a clear liquid” alongside students who showed up at her home at 10 p.m. with a bottle of vodka.

articles.title: Trump news at a glance: US fires on Iranian-flagged tanker as president fires off post threatening more bombing
articles.description: Trump warned Tehran to agree to deal or face attacks ‘at a much higher level and intensity’ than before – key US politics stories from 6 May 2026 at a glance The US military fired on an Iranian-flagged oil tanker on Wednesday, shortly after Donald Trump issued a fresh ultimatum to Tehran, telling it to accept a deal to end the war or face a new wave of US bombing “at a much higher level and intensity than it was before”. The US fighter fired several rounds and “disabled the tanker’s rudder” as it attempted to breach the US’s blockade of Iranian ports, US Central Command said in a social media post. Continue reading...

articles.title: Man accused of threatening deputy mayor was convicted for murdering priest
articles.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) -- A Buffalo man accused of threatening the city's deputy mayor with a knife last week was convicted of murdering a priest in Buffalo more than 40 years ago. William Royle, 67, is accused of making the threat against Deputy Mayor Eugenio Russi near Court Street on Friday, officials said. Russi, one of [...]

articles.title: 6 hurt in Des Plaines blaze
articles.description:
Six people suffered injuries in an apartment fire in Des Plaines early Wednesday morning, according to multiple media reports.

Des Plaines fire officials have yet to release any details about the blaze at the three-story apartment complex on the 1200 block of Harding Avenue.

The extent of injuries was unknown, but witnesses reported hearing an explosion before the fire erupted, according to a WGN news report.

For more on this story, please visit DailyHerald.com.
